name: Waking Up
epithet: The first words of the day
meta: What to say when you wake up (adhkar al-istiyqadh): the praise, the dhikr of the night, and the verses the Prophet ﷺ recited on waking. Arabic, transliteration, meaning, source.

name: Morning Adhkar
epithet: The remembrances that open your day
meta: The complete morning adhkar (adhkar as-sabah) in Arabic, transliteration and English, each with its source and how many times to recite. Qur'an verified via quran.ai, hadith from Hisn al-Muslim.

name: After Salah
epithet: The dhikr that follows every prayer
meta: The adhkar after salah (adhkar ba'd as-salah): istighfar, the tasbih of 33, Ayat al-Kursi and more, each with Arabic, transliteration, meaning, source and count. Qur'an verified via quran.ai.

name: Evening Adhkar
epithet: The remembrances to close your day
meta: The evening adhkar (adhkar al-masa): the same protective remembrances as the morning, recited from after Asr until sunset. Arabic, transliteration, meaning, source and count. Qur'an via quran.ai.

name: Before Sleep
epithet: The night's protection
meta: The adhkar before sleep (adhkar an-nawm): the three Quls, Ayat al-Kursi, the last verses of al-Baqarah, the tasbih of Fatimah and the last words of the night. Arabic, transliteration, meaning, source.

name: Salawat
epithet: Blessings upon the Prophet ﷺ
meta: The salawat (durood) the Prophet ﷺ taught: the full Ibrahimi blessing recited in every prayer, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning, with its source. Verified from Hisn al-Muslim.

name: Praises of Allah
epithet: The words light on the tongue, heavy on the scale
meta: The praises of Allah (tasbih, tahmid, tahlil, takbir): SubhanAllah wa bihamdihi, La ilaha illallah, the four most beloved words and al-baqiyat as-salihat, with Arabic, meaning, virtue and source.

name: Istighfar
epithet: Turning back to Allah
meta: Istighfar (seeking forgiveness): Sayyid al-Istighfar (the chief of seeking forgiveness), Astaghfirullah wa atubu ilayh, and more, with Arabic, transliteration, meaning and source. From Hisn al-Muslim.

name: Qur'anic Du'as
epithet: The supplications Allah taught in His Book
meta: The du'as of the Qur'an (the Rabbana supplications): for good in both worlds, steadfast prayer, a righteous family, forgiveness and more. Arabic and Saheeh International, verified via quran.ai.

name: Ruqyah & Illness
epithet: Healing words for the sick, for your own pain, and against the evil eye
meta: The ruqyah for illness: al-Fatihah, Ayat al-Kursi and the three Quls, with the prophetic du'as for the sick, for your own pain, and against the evil eye. Arabic, transliteration and meaning.

name: Sunnah Du'as
epithet: The du'as the Prophet ﷺ carried
meta: The heart-supplications the Prophet ﷺ carried: for anxiety and grief, after sinning, at wonder and joy, in Arabic, transliteration and English with sources.

name: Wudu & Bathroom
epithet: Remembrance at the edge of purity
meta: Short du'as for entering and leaving the bathroom, and the words to say before and after wudu, in Arabic, transliteration and English, each with its source.

name: Leaving & Entering the Home
epithet: Words for the doorway
meta: The du'as for leaving and entering your home, in Arabic, transliteration and English, each with its source. Step out in Allah's protection and return in His Name.

name: The Masjid & the Adhan
epithet: Answering the call to the house of Allah
meta: The du'as for walking to the masjid, entering and leaving it, and what to say with the adhan, in Arabic, transliteration and English, each with its source.

name: Food & Drink
epithet: The remembrances of the table
meta: The sunnah du'as of food and drink: Bismillah before eating, alhamdulillah after, the du'a for your host, and the words for breaking a fast. Arabic, transliteration, meaning and source.

name: Travel
epithet: The remembrances of the road
meta: The sunnah du'as of travel: mounting your transport, setting out, entering a town, bidding farewell, and returning home. Arabic, transliteration, meaning and source, verified.

name: The Prayer of Istikharah
epithet: Asking Allah to choose for you when you cannot see which way is best
meta: How to pray istikharah, the prayer for seeking Allah's guidance in a decision: the two rak'ahs and the full du'a in Arabic, transliteration and English. Via Buruja.

name: Gatherings
epithet: What to say while you sit, and the words that seal it
meta: Authentic du'as for any gathering: the seeking of forgiveness the Prophet ﷺ repeated as he sat, and kaffarat al-majlis, the expiation said before you rise.

name: Difficulty & Distress
epithet: The words the Prophet ﷺ turned to when the weight came down
meta: Authentic du'as for anxiety, grief, severe distress and hard times, in Arabic, transliteration and English, each with its source. From the sunnah, via Buruja.

name: Nature & Weather
epithet: The remembrances for wind, rain, and the turning sky
meta: Authentic du'as for wind, thunder, seeking rain, when it rains, after rain, and the new moon, in Arabic, transliteration and English, each with its source.

name: For Other People
epithet: The words of good companionship
meta: Authentic du'as to say to and for other people: thanking with jazak Allahu khayran, blessing a host, loving for Allah's sake, and what to say when praised.

name: Protecting Your Faith
epithet: What to do when doubt whispers, and how to drive Shaytan away
meta: What the Prophet ﷺ taught for whispers of doubt about faith and for repelling Shaytan, in Arabic, transliteration and English. From the sunnah, via Buruja.

name: Hajj & Umrah
epithet: The adhkar of the pilgrim through the rites
meta: The pilgrim's adhkar through the rites of Hajj and Umrah: talbiyah, tawaf, sa'i, Arafah, Muzdalifah and the jamarat, in Arabic, transliteration and English with sources.

name: Money & Shopping
epithet: Keeping Allah in mind where the world is loudest
meta: Du'as for entering the market, seeking sufficiency through the lawful, and relief from debt, in Arabic, transliteration and English, each with its source and grading.

name: Marriage & Children
epithet: The du'as for the home you are building
meta: Du'as for the newly married, before intimacy, congratulating a newborn, and seeking Allah's protection for your children, in Arabic, transliteration and English with sources.

name: Death & Grief
epithet: What to say when someone is dying, when they pass, and after
meta: Du'as for death and grief: comforting the dying, what to say when someone passes, the funeral prayer, condolence and visiting graves. Arabic, transliteration and meaning.
